statement.en: Meaningful Change Index: A P-Value Independent Metric for Assessing Robustness and Fragility in Continuous Outcomes
statement.summary: The assessment of statistical robustness in clinical trials with continuous outcomes has relied heavily on p-value dependent metrics that fail to address clinically meaningful differences. This study introduces the Meaningful Change Index - MeCI - a novel metric applied to continuous data that quantifies how distinguishable treatment groups are based on the crossover point of their sample distributions. It is independent of statistical significance testing. The Meaningful Change Index is defined as the minimum distance from group means to their distributional crossover point, normalized by the combined standard deviations. Unlike existing continuous fragility indices that depend on p-value thresholds, Meaningful Change Index focuses on a statistically meaningful numeric separation between

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The proposed Meaningful Change Index (MeCI) is a novel metric under initial validation. While the core mathematical construct (distance to distributional crossover normalized by combined SDs) is formalizable, its clinical validity, comparative performance against established fragility indices, and generalization properties remain empirically unverified. The abstract indicates work-in-progress status (preprint, 2026 dating suggests recent submission). Claims of p-value independence and clinical meaningfulness require validation through broader datasets and peer review.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 分布が出会う地点から、平均がどれだけ遠く立っているかが、本当の違いである。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 二つの丘がそれぞれの頂点から交差点へ向かう距離が、真なる意味を測る。
